topStories.summary: Precision engineering firm Indo-MIM is set to launch its IPO later this month, targeting around $420 million after trimming the issue size from an earlier $700 million plan. The offering, delayed due to Middle East tensions, comes amid improving market sentiment and renewed foreign investor interest in Indian equities.

topStories.title: Quote of the day by Jean Marie Eveillard: "Risk to us goes back to not paying attention to how one does in the short term"
topStories.summary: Value investor Jean-Marie Eveillard emphasizes long-term focus over short-term market volatility. He argues that focusing on daily price movements leads to emotional and wealth-eroding decisions. Patience and business quality are key competitive advantages for disciplined investors. History shows markets move in cycles, rewarding those who stay the course. Greatest risk lies in letting short-term noise dictate long-term investment choices.

topStories.title: Market wrap: Top gainers and losers on Nifty and Sensex today
topStories.summary: Indian benchmark indices ended sharply lower on Tuesday as escalating Middle East tensions and surging crude oil prices triggered broad-based selling. Sensex fell 561 points and Nifty slipped below 24,100, with auto, IT, PSU banks and realty leading losses, while pharma stocks outperformed amid a flight to defensive sectors.

topStories.summary: On Tuesday, Indian government bonds faced a notable downturn, with the benchmark 10-year yield climbing to a three-week peak as global market anxiety intensified. The recent spike in oil prices following tensions in the U.S.-Iran relationship has shaken investor confidence, resulting in a weaker rupee and declining stock values in Mumbai. Attention now turns to an impending decision on including Indian bonds in the global index.

topStories.title: Moody's affirms SBI, HDFC Bank ratings on strong asset quality
topStories.summary: Moody's Ratings has confirmed the ratings for both State Bank of India and HDFC Bank, attributing a stable outlook to their robust asset quality. SBI excels with its diverse lending practices and stable net interest margins, while HDFC Bank is bolstered by a strong retail presence that ensures a low-cost deposit base. The agency forecasts that asset quality for both banks will remain stable moving forward.

This initiative helps rebuild the wetland's ecological network and biodiversity.

india.title: Astronomers have spent decades searching nearby stars for another Earth, and now they may have found one just 25 light-years away
india.summary: Scientists have found GJ 3378b, a planet near Earth with potential for life. This exoplanet is twice Earth's size and orbits within its star's habitable zone. Researchers used ground-based instruments to update measurements of GJ 3378b's mass. Future telescopes will study its atmosphere to confirm habitability and search for biosignatures. This discovery adds GJ 3378b to the list of promising nearby exoplanets.

india.title: Why Oklahoma speed-limit signs say “No tolerance,” and what it really means for drivers
india.summary: Oklahoma's "No Tolerance" signs remind drivers that posted speed limits are absolute. These signs reinforce the state's strict approach to speeding enforcement and traffic safety. Legally, exceeding the speed limit by even one mile per hour is a violation. Law enforcement officers still use discretion in issuing citations for minor infractions. Drivers should treat posted speeds as the actual limit to avoid penalties.

india.title: Rare fossil reveals T. rex’s attack style
india.summary: An amazing fossil discovery shows a T. rex tooth stuck in an Edmontosaurus skull, dating back 66 million years. This proves a direct clash between these dinosaurs. The tooth confirms T. rex involvement, meaning the Edmontosaurus was either attacked or scavenged. This is crucial evidence for understanding T. rex's hunting habits.

india.title: Meet Carol Ruckdeschel: 84-year-old 'wildest woman in America' who has been living on an island for the 53 years in order to preserve nature for the future
india.summary: Carol Ruckdeschel has lived on Cumberland Island for 53 years, preserving its natural state. She meticulously documents the island's diverse flora and fauna in her field journals. Her dedication led to legislative changes protecting sea turtles from shrimping trawlers. Ruckdeschel sustains herself by living off the land and off the grid. She continues to advocate for the island's preservation against development proposals.
